name: answers codebase questions
response: <http://shadow.ai|shadow.ai> is a Slack bot that runs Claude Code on your local machine. Here's what it can do:  *Core Capabilities:* • *Slack messaging* — responds in Slack threads, monitors channels • *MCP tool access* — connects to external services like Jira, Azure DevOps, Sentry, Grafana, MongoDB, New Relic, GCP logging, and more via MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers • *Knowledge notes* — persistent file-based memory system that remembers context across conversations • *Channel monitoring* — watches Slack channels for relevant messages • *Custom agents* — specialized subagents for tasks like PR reviews, debugging, incident reports, test writing, and sandbox deployments • *Skills* — slash-command workflows for things like `/azure-review`, `/kube-login`, `/commit`, TDD, brainstorming, and more  *What it is NOT:* It's not a platform with built-in databases or monitoring. It *accesses* tools like Grafana, Sentry, MongoDB, and New Relic through configured MCP integrations — it doesn't host them.  *Typical use cases:* • Review PRs on Azure DevOps • Look up and update Jira tickets • Debug issues using Sentry, logs, and traces • Deploy branches to sandbox environments • Write and run integration tests • Explore and modify codebases • Create incident reports  Think of it as Claude Code running locally, with a Slack interface and a rich set of integrations for your dev workflow.  _sent by shadow.ai_

quality_scores.feedback: Response is accurate, comprehensive, and well-formatted with strong Slack presentation, but runs longer than necessary for a basic 'what is this bot' question — could trim redundant sections without losing clarity.
